description: A small, curated reference sample describing what well-run, security-conscious domains typically publish under /.well-known/. This is illustrative editorial context to help a reader judge a scan result, not a statistical population — do not cite these numbers as measured survey data. See README.md's Design Principles section and the methodology field below for how it was assembled.
methodology: Compiled by editorial judgment informed by publicly observable practice among domains known for maintained public vulnerability disclosure programs, rounded to the nearest 5%. To refresh with real measurements: maintain a domain list in docs/reference-corpus-domains.txt, run `magpie -f docs/reference-corpus-domains.txt --json --no-timestamps`, compute the percentage of domains where each path's presence is "present", and replace the percent_present values below along with the 'updated' date and 'sample_size'.

paths.note: The strongest single signal of a maintained disclosure program.
Near-universal; not itself a security maturity signal.
Common among domains operating their own mail infrastructure.
Growing password-manager convention, still far from universal.
Present only on domains running federated authentication.
Present only on domains running a standalone OAuth authorization server.
Present only on domains with a companion Android app.
Present only on domains with a companion iOS app.
Largely legacy; rarely published on new infrastructure.
Do Not Track has fallen out of common practice.
Common on domains that are also installable web apps, not itself a security signal.
